behaviors.description: Missing required key across file+env reports exact dotted config path in the actionable error.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: local module wiring (file config + env override + validation/error formatting). The failure can appear at the handoff between source-merging and validator path reporting, so Unit is often too narrow for this behavior. Pass 2 mapping: Integration. Integration-brake: (a) no real network/subprocess/OS primitive is required; (b) no AI/ML output; (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejected higher level: behavior text is only about config-source presence and message content, not real external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp config file missing `database.host`, clear matching `TOOL_` env override, run the real loader entrypoint, and assert it fails with an error that contains exactly `database.host`.

behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: pure path-resolution logic from environment inputs. The behavior text is specifically about choosing one path base over another, not reading/writing files. Pass 2 mapping: Unit. Rejected higher levels: no required module boundary beyond resolver logic, and behavior text does not require filesystem, subprocess, network, or model output.
behaviors.test_description: Call the path-resolution function with `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` set and assert returned config path is `<XDG_CONFIG_HOME>/tool/config.yaml`; with it unset, assert fallback is `~/.config/tool/config.yaml`.

behaviors.description: `TOOL_` environment variables take precedence over conflicting YAML file keys.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: module interaction between YAML loader, env key mapping (`TOOL_` prefix), and precedence merge logic. This failure is typically at source-combination boundaries, not a single isolated function assumption. Pass 2 mapping: Integration. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess behavior required beyond in-process env + local file; (b) no AI/ML dependency; (c) no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejected higher level: behavior text is deterministic precedence, not external-system semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Write YAML with a key (e.g., `database.host: filehost`), set `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=envhost`, run loader, and assert effective config uses `envhost`.

behaviors.description: YAML syntax errors report the parser-detected line number in the user-facing error.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: integration with real YAML parser error objects plus local loader error formatting. Mocking parser exceptions can hide real line/column shape differences, so real parser invocation is needed. Pass 2 mapping: Integration. Integration-brake: (a) no subprocess/real network/OS-only primitive needed; (b) no AI/ML; (c) no multi-agentic sequence. Rejected System: behavior text is about YAML parse diagnostics, which can be reproduced with in-process parser + local file/string input.
behaviors.test_description: Provide invalid YAML in config (with known error line), run loader, assert failure message includes that line number from parser output.

behaviors.description: On first run with no config directory, loader creates directory and writes defaults before continuing.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: local filesystem side effects across loader + defaults writer modules. A pure unit fake can miss path wiring/order bugs, so an in-process integration test with temp filesystem is the minimum. Pass 2 mapping: Integration. Integration-brake: (a) no required real network/subprocess or OS event primitive beyond normal local filesystem operations; (b) no AI/ML dependency; (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejected System: behavior text only requires create-dir/write-file semantics, which temp-dir integration tests can expose.
behaviors.test_description: Start with a temp base where config directory is absent, run startup loader, assert directory is created, defaults file exists at expected path, and loader proceeds without fatal error.
